title: bufonia ephedrina subsp. ephedrina
intro: Bufonia ephedrina subsp. ephedrina, also known as Ephedra, is a type of small, shrub-like plant that belongs to the Ephedraceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to stimulate the nervous system and increase circulation, which is attributed to the presence of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine alkaloids. The health benefits of Bufonia ephedrina subsp. ephedrina include relief from respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as assistance in weight loss and improved mental clarity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, capsules, or tablets, although its use is regulated in some countries due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
